audienceSections.normal: This chapter teaches [[grammar:sinds + starting point]] and [[grammar:present tense + al + duration]]. Use [[grammar:sinds]] before a date, year, or event that began the situation. Use the present tense with [[grammar:al]] to say how long something has been true.
audienceSections.expert: This chapter introduces [[grammar:sinds + starting point]]: [[grammar:Sinds]] establishes a left boundary while the present tense presents the situation as continuing.; [[grammar:present tense + al + duration]]: Dutch commonly uses the present for a state extending from the past through now, with [[grammar:al]] expressing duration.

audienceSections.normal:
#### sinds + starting point

Use [[grammar:sinds]] before a date, year, or event that began the situation. Example: [[grammar:Fatima woont sinds haar verhuizing in deze buurt.]]

#### present tense + al + duration

Use the present tense with [[grammar:al]] to say how long something has been true. Example: [[grammar:Opa Henk woont hier al veertig jaar.]]
audienceSections.expert:
#### sinds + starting point

[[grammar:Sinds]] establishes a left boundary while the present tense presents the situation as continuing. Literal example: [[grammar:Fatima woont sinds haar verhuizing in deze buurt.]]

#### present tense + al + duration

Dutch commonly uses the present for a state extending from the past through now, with [[grammar:al]] expressing duration. Literal example: [[grammar:Opa Henk woont hier al veertig jaar.]]

audienceSections.normal: [[grammar:Sinds]] marks the beginning of a continuing situation. Dutch uses the present with [[grammar:al]] plus duration for a state continuing through now.
audienceSections.expert: [[grammar:Sinds]] marks the beginning of a continuing situation. Dutch uses the present with [[grammar:al]] plus duration for a state continuing through now. The grammar IDs remain distinct and cumulative.

breakdown.normal:
- [[grammar:Fatima woont sinds haar verhuizing in deze buurt.]] — Fatima has lived in this neighborhood since her move.
- [[grammar:Opa Henk woont hier al veertig jaar.]] — Grandpa Henk has lived here for forty years.
- [[grammar:Pieter kent elke buurman en buurvrouw in de straat.]] — Pieter knows every male and female neighbor in the street.
- [[grammar:Yasmin woont sinds haar studie dicht bij het café.]] — Yasmin has lived near the café since her studies.
- [[grammar:De bewoners onderhouden samen een gemeenschappelijke tuin.]] — The residents maintain a communal garden together.
- [[grammar:Fatima werkt al vijf jaar bij de medische post.]] — Fatima has worked at the medical station for five years.
- [[grammar:Opa Henk ontmoet nieuwe bewoners tijdens elk buurtfeest.]] — Grandpa Henk meets new residents during every neighborhood party.
- [[grammar:Pieter organiseert het feest al sinds de eerste editie.]] — Pieter has organized the party since the first edition.
- [[grammar:In de buurt wonen vierduizendvierhonderdeen mensen.]] — Four thousand four hundred one people live in the neighborhood.
- [[grammar:De gemeenschappelijke zaal is sinds vorig jaar groter.]] — The communal hall has been larger since last year.
- [[grammar:Yasmin kent haar buurvrouw al drie jaar.]] — Yasmin has known her neighbor for three years.
- [[grammar:Fatima onderhoudt contact met oudere bewoners.]] — Fatima maintains contact with older residents.
- [[grammar:Sinds de renovatie komen meer families naar de tuin.]] — Since the renovation, more families come to the garden.
- [[grammar:Opa Henk vertelt hoe de buurt vroeger was.]] — Grandpa Henk tells how the neighborhood used to be.
- [[grammar:Niet iedereen woont er even lang, maar iedereen helpt.]] — Not everyone has lived there for the same length of time, but everyone helps.
- [[grammar:Daardoor voelt de buurt nog steeds vertrouwd.]] — As a result, the neighborhood still feels familiar.
